commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4e167e9b8dc99eee5f83d340075715323cb10b10)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `configure` and `onboard` to the CLI respawn skip path with unit coverage so interactive setup commands bypass warning-suppression child respawn.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not fully: the source path and linked Debian PTY report are strong, but I did not run a current-main live PTY reproduction of the high-CPU hang.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor or maintainer action for rebase plus real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not open a repair lane while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(cli): skip respawn for interactive TTY commands (configure, onboard)
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw configure` and `openclaw onboard` hang and leave high-CPU child processes on some Linux kernel/Node.js combinations (e.g. Debian kernel 6.12.63 + Node 22.22.1). Reported in #83560.

### Root Cause

The ExperimentalWarning suppression path in `entry.ts` (the `ensureExperimentalWarningSuppressed` function) spawns a child process with `stdio: 'inherit'` and then installs a signal bridge via `attachChildProcessBridge`. This creates a parent/child pair that shares the same TTY fd:

- Parent: sits in `epoll_wait` / event loop with active signal handlers (~34% CPU in repro)
- Child: clack `select()` calls `stdin.setRawMode(true)` on the shared TTY fd → busy-waits at high CPU (~86%) or hangs

`shouldSkipRespawnForArgv` already returns `true` for `--help` / `--version` to bypass this path; the interactive subcommands were never added to that list.

## Fix

Add `configure` and `onboard` to a new `INTERACTIVE_TTY_COMMANDS` set in `respawn-policy.ts`. When `shouldSkipRespawnForArgv` detects either as `argv[2]` (the primary subcommand position), it returns `true` and the respawn wrapper is skipped entirely. The ExperimentalWarning may be visible in output, which is an acceptable trade-off for correct interactive behaviour.

## Changes

- `src/cli/respawn-policy.ts`: add `INTERACTIVE_TTY_COMMANDS` set and `hasInteractiveTtyCommand` helper; extend `shouldSkipRespawnForArgv`
- `src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts`: add tests for `configure` / `onboard` skip, and a guard test ensuring the skip only applies when the command is the primary subcommand (`argv[2]`)

## Testing

```
vitest run src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts   # 14 tests, all pass
```

### Acceptance criteria (for live repro)
```bash
timeout -s TERM -k 2s 12s script -qefc 'TERM=xterm-256color openclaw configure' /tmp/oc-test.log
# Must not hang; must not leave high-CPU child processes after exit
```

Fixes #83560
target.author: lml2468
